doc_title: On the performativity of SDG classifications in large bibliometric databases
evidence: ["Su, Y. and J. Xu (2022). An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding for open-ended text generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2211.10797."]

Group, University of Granada, Granada, Spain May 7, 2024 --- Round 3 --- I see that the system returned DOC1_A1 (the title/author header) instead of DOC2's references. This means I need to check what anchors are available for DOC2. Let me look at the document map for DOC2 and DOC3 to understand their structure. Actually, looking back at the task, I only have the anchor map for DOC1 provided in the initial context. I don't see the anchor maps for DOC2 and DOC3 in the current trace. The system should have provided these, but they're not visible in what I can see. Let me reconsider - I need to find references from DOC2 and DOC3 to build the citation chain. From DOC1_A14, I found that DOC1 cites: - "Su, Y. and J. Xu (2022). An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding for open-ended text generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2211.10797." This matches DOC3's title "An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation" So DOC1 → DOC3 (DOC1 cites DOC3) Now I need to check if DOC3 cites DOC2, or if DOC2 cites DOC3, or if there's another relationship. Since I don't have the anchor maps for DOC2 and DOC3 visible, let me try to open what I think would be the references section. Typically references are in the last anchor. Let me try DOC2_A14 (assuming similar structure to DOC1). Actually, wait - the system gave me DOC1_A1 instead of DOC2_A14. This suggests that DOC2_A14 might not exist or the anchor ID is different. Let me try a different approach - I should check what anchors exist for DOC2. Since I can't see DOC2's anchor map, let me try opening DOC2_A1 first to see the document structure, then I can identify the references section. {"action":"open","anchor_id":"DOC2_A1","expected_evidence":"Title and structure of DOC2 to understand its anchor organization"} #### Abstract Large bibliometric databases, such as Web of Science, Scopus, and OpenAlex, facilitate bibliometric analyses, but are performative, affecting the visibility of scientific outputs and the impact measurement of participating entities. Recently, these databases have taken up the UN's Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in their respective classifications, which have been criticised for their diverging nature. This work proposes using the feature of large language models (LLMs) to learn about the "data bias" injected by diverse SDG classifications into bibliometric data by exploring five SDGs. We build a LLM that is fine-tuned in parallel by the diverse SDG classifications inscribed into the databases' SDG classifications. Our results show high sensitivity in model architecture, classified publications, fine-tuning process, and natural language generation. The wide arbitrariness at different levels raises concerns about using LLM in research practice. Keywords: Sustainable Development Goals, Classifications, Bibliometric databases, Large Language Models, Text Analysis, Noun Phrases, OpenAlex, Web of Science, Scopus.[^0] ## 1 Introduction --- Round 4 --- The system is returning DOC1 anchors instead of DOC2 anchors. This is strange - when I requested DOC2_A1, I got DOC1_A2 instead. It seems like the system might not have loaded DOC2 and DOC3 properly, or there's an issue with the anchor IDs. Let me reconsider the situation. I've only been able to access DOC1's content. From DOC1_A14 (the references section), I found that DOC1 cites: - "Su, Y. and J. Xu (2022). An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding for open-ended text generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2211.10797." This matches DOC3's title "An Empirical Study On Contrastive Search And Contrastive Decoding For Open-ended Text Generation" So I have evidence that DOC1 → DOC3 (DOC1 cites DOC3). However, I cannot access DOC2 or DOC3's content to check their references. The system keeps returning DOC1 anchors when I try to access DOC2 anchors. Given the constraints: 1. I need to check at least one anchor before stopping (I've opened DOC1_A14 and DOC1_A1 and DOC1_A2) 2. I cannot fabricate evidence 3. I should try to capture as much evidence as possible from the documents I can access Since I can only access DOC1's content, I should report what I found from DOC1 and stop. The citation relationship I found is: - DOC1 cites DOC3 (based on the reference "Su, Y. and J. Xu (2022). An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding for open-ended text generation") I cannot verify if DOC3 cites DOC2 or if DOC2 cites any of the other papers because I cannot access DOC2 or DOC3's content. Let me stop and report the evidence I found from DOC1. {"action":"stop","reason":"Unable to access DOC2 and DOC3 content - system returns DOC1 anchors when requesting DOC2/DOC3 anchors. Only evidence available is from DOC1 references showing DOC1 cites DOC3."} ["Su, Y. and J. Xu (2022). An empirical study on contrastive search and contrastive decoding for open-ended text generation. arXiv preprint arXiv:2211.10797."]
